steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the passage. The passage describes Padre Florentino playing sad music alone — and ends with the decisive phrase: he "gave free rein to the sadness in his heart." The music and his emotions are directly linked.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The residence of Padre Florentino is especially isolated.’: The setting is described (sea, shore, nearby woods), but isolation is a backdrop, not what the passage centers on. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Padre Florentino prefers to play music rather than listen to other musicians play.’: No comparison between playing and listening appears anywhere in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Padre Florentino has dedicated himself to becoming a skilled musician.’: He is called an "accomplished musician," but the passage doesn't depict a journey toward skill — it depicts emotional expression through improvisation. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘The sadness of Padre Florentino's music reflects his own feelings.’: The music is sad, and the final sentence explains why: he gave free rein to the sadness in his heart. The music is an outlet for his inner state. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
